title: Font
description: You can easily change fonts as long as you have the font file (.ttf file).
body:
# Font
Font is a client-side Fabric mod.

It allows you to replace the default in-game font with your favorite font file (.ttf/.otf).

Simply place the font file in the folder and select it in-game to apply it as the runtime font.

Please obtain the font from [``Fonts.Google.com``](https://fonts.google.com/) or similar sources.

## Features

- Replaces Minecraft's default font with a custom font
- Supports both .ttf and .otf files
- Automatically detects files in the font folder
- Preview fonts before applying
- Adjust the size and toggle bold for each font
- Disable custom fonts to instantly revert to vanilla fonts
- Uses Minecraft's fallback font for missing characters
## How to Use

1. By default, the font manager opens with `F7`. You can also open it from the Mod menu.

2. You can change the key to open it in the mod's key settings if needed. 3. Click "Open Folder" and place your .ttf/.otf file in the font folder.

4. If the game is already running, click "Refresh List". 5. Select the font you want to use from the list.

6. Adjust the size and bold settings.

7. Check the preview.

8. Press "Done" to save and apply the selected font.

## Font Folder

The default folder is as follows:

- `<Game Directory>/Font/Fonts`

## Key Controls

- The default font key is `F7`.

- The font key can be changed from the MOD settings screen.

## Notes

- If the active font is deleted or becomes unreadable, it will be automatically disabled.

- Changes made in the manager will not be applied until you press "Done".

- If some characters are missing, you can use Minecraft's default font and unifonts as alternatives.
